group.name: Which SexLab Framework do you run?

plugin.name: SexLab Framework P+ (2.x)
plugin.description:
Default. For SexLab Framework P+ 2.17 or newer (the SLPP fork with SexLab Scene Builder and node-collision physics).
This build uses the full feature set: per-stage/per-position scene annotations, measured thrust-speed intensity (the F/S label prefix), physics-driven contact SFX, and SLSB climax annotations.
Requires: SexLab Framework P+ 2.17+.

plugin.name: SexLab Framework SE 1.63 (classic) + SLSO
plugin.description:
For classic SexLab Framework SE 1.63. Installs the classic script set, which replaces the six framework-facing scripts.
THIS OPTION HAS TWO EXTRA HARD REQUIREMENTS:
1. SexLab Separate Orgasm (SLSO). Classic SexLab only fires a per-thread orgasm event; SLSO republishes the per-actor event that SLO VE's voice, expressions, SFX and milk triggers all key off. Let SLSO overwrite SexLab - its sslActorAlias override is what emits that event.
2. SLATE plus a Hentairim-convention tag database (e.g. "Hentairim tags.json" in SKSE\Plugins\SLATE). SLATE applies the per-stage/per-position label codes (1ASVP, 5BENI, ...) as SexLab animation tags, and those tags ARE the label engine on classic. Without a tag database every label falls back to lead-in and you will hear only generic behaviour.
With both installed, label fidelity matches the P+ build. The one genuine difference is that classic has no node-collision physics, so physics-driven contact SFX and the adaptive-velocity search are disabled (tag- and timer-driven body SFX work normally), and the fast/slow intensity prefix comes from the authored tag rather than measured thrust speed.
Install order: SexLab 1.63 - SLSO - SLATE + tags - AudioUtil - voice packs - SLO VE last.
